title: Git Basics: Init, Add, Commit, Status, and .gitignore
content:
### Day 38: Git Basics: Init, Add, Commit, Status, and .gitignore

**Git** tracks code changes. Every product company uses it.

**Core Commands:** git init, git add, git commit, git status, git log.

**.gitignore:** List files Git should never track (data files, secrets, __pycache__).
codeExample:
# Day 38: Git Basics: Init, Add, Commit, Status, and .gitignore
# Git Commands (run in terminal)

# Initialize repo
# git init

# Check status
# git status

# Stage files
# git add .                    # all files
# git add src/model.py         # specific file

# Commit
# git commit -m "Add churn prediction model"

# View history
# git log --oneline -5

# .gitignore for ML projects
gitignore = """# Python
__pycache__/
*.pyc
.env
venv/

# Data
*.csv
*.pkl
data/raw/

# IDE
.vscode/
.idea/

# Notebooks
.ipynb_checkpoints/"""
print(gitignore)
practiceQuestions: What is git init, add, commit?
keyTakeaways: Core Commands: git init, git add, git commit, git status, git log.
.gitignore: List files Git should never track (data files, secrets, __pycache__).
